**Epic:** GitHub-tracking integration · **Sprint:** Sprint 3 - GitHub integration · **Effort:** Medium · **Priority:** P0 - Critical

Gives Claude a one-call snapshot of the linked repo (open issues, PRs, milestones with due dates, recent commits, optional Projects v2 board columns) to drive coaching without requiring multiple back-and-forth tool calls.

## Acceptance criteria
- get_project_overview(goalId) resolves Goal.githubRepo; if field is null or goal not found, returns errorResult('No GitHub repo linked — call link_github_project first.').
- Makes the following GitHub API calls in parallel where safe (repo metadata, open issues count, milestones, recent commits, open PRs) — all via REST GET to https://api.github.com except the optional Projects v2 GraphQL call.
- If Goal.githubProjectNumber is set, makes exactly ONE GraphQL POST to https://api.github.com/graphql for Projects v2 board columns; if not set, projectBoard is null in the response.
- Returns the documented shape: { repo, defaultBranch, openIssues, openPRs, milestones: [{number,title,dueOn,openIssues,closedIssues,state}], recentCommits: [{sha,message,date,author}] (last 5), projectBoard?: {columns:[{name,cardCount}]}|null, rateLimitRemaining: number }.
- rateLimitRemaining is extracted from the X-RateLimit-Remaining response header of any REST call and included in the jsonResult — allows Claude to self-moderate on heavy sessions.
- Token sanitization: a safeGitHub(fn) helper (or equivalent pattern) wraps all GitHub fetch logic; any error thrown inside is caught, and the message is sanitized with message.replaceAll(process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN ?? 'REDACTED', '[REDACTED]') before being passed to errorResult. The generic safe() wrapper is NOT relied upon for this sanitization.
- If the repo is private/inaccessible or the token is wrong, tool returns a sanitized errorResult (not a 500); the raw GitHub 401/403/404 body is sanitized before surfacing.
- GITHUB_TOKEN is never present in any jsonResult field — verified by grepping the returned JSON for the actual token value in a smoke test.

content.body:
**Epic:** GitHub-tracking integration · **Sprint:** Sprint 3 - GitHub integration · **Effort:** Medium · **Priority:** P0 - Critical

Mirrors GitHub milestones into the ScheduledItem table so Claude and the UI can reason about the project timeline without making a live GitHub call on every coaching turn.

## Acceptance criteria
- sync_github_milestones(goalId, closeCompleted?) fetches all milestones from GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/milestones?state=open (and optionally state=closed when closeCompleted=true) via the GitHub REST API.
- For each milestone, externalRef is set to 'gh:milestone:<milestone_number>' (e.g. 'gh:milestone:3').
- Upsert logic: prisma.scheduledItem.upsert({ where: { goalId_externalRef: { goalId, externalRef } }, update: { title, detail, date, payload }, create: { goalId, externalRef, type: 'milestone', title, detail, date, payload, status: 'planned' } }). Re-running creates 0 new rows — idempotency verified by running twice and comparing count before/after.
- UTC date bucketing: GitHub milestone due_on is an ISO string (e.g. '2026-06-15T07:00:00Z'). The due date is extracted as due_on.slice(0, 10) (the yyyy-mm-dd portion) and passed through startOfDay(parseDateKey(dateStr)) from @/lib/calendar. Never use new Date(due_on) directly — this would produce a UTC midnight, which is yesterday in MT.
- Milestones with no due_on (null) are synced with date set to a sentinel far-future date (e.g. new Date('2099-12-31')) or skipped — decision must be made explicit in the implementation (not left to chance). Document in code comment.
- payload field on each ScheduledItem stores the raw GitHub milestone object (number, title, description, due_on, open_issues, closed_issues, state) as Json.
- Returns { synced: number, updated: number, skipped: number, items: [{ externalRef, title, date: string }] }.
- If closeCompleted is true, milestones in GitHub state='closed' are upserted with status='done' and completedAt set to the GitHub milestone's closed_at timestamp.
- Token sanitization: uses safeGitHub pattern; GITHUB_TOKEN absent from all returned fields and error messages.
- tsc --noEmit and npm run build pass.
- Smoke test: call sync_github_milestones twice in a row; second call returns synced=0 new rows (idempotency confirmed); existing ScheduledItems are updated, not duplicated.
- Connector reload note.

## Touches
`src/lib/mcp/tools.ts (or github-tools.ts) — add sync_github_milestones`, `Uses prisma.scheduledItem.upsert — imports from @/lib/db`, `Uses parseDateKey + startOfDay from @/lib/calendar for UTC→USER_TZ date bucketing`

## Depends on
- C-1 (link_github_project — Goal.githubRepo must be set)
- B-1 (ScheduledItem table + @@unique([goalId,externalRef]) constraint exist from Epic A/B; schedule_item write path available as a pattern)

content.body:
**Epic:** Chewgether goal MVP + coaching · **Sprint:** Backlog · **Effort:** Large · **Priority:** P3 - Low

Let a non-operator user stand up and feed a goal from within the app (or a guided claude.ai flow) without the operator running a seed script. Two capabilities under one roof:

## A. Self-serve goal + milestone seeding (original scope)
Seed a goal and its milestones in-app — removing the `prisma/seed-chewgether.ts` manual step.

## B. Apply-a-doc-to-a-goal (folded in 2026-06-29)
A user has strategy/plan docs (e.g. the rhino.the.grey Brand Canon / Content Roadmap / Angle Bank) and wants to "implement and execute" them through a goal. Today this is fully **coach-mediated via MCP** — the user shares the docs in claude.ai, the coach reads them and writes references + scheduled items by hand (done manually for rhino on 2026-06-28). The primitives already exist (`add_goal_reference`, `update_goal_reference`, `schedule_item`, `update_goal_targets`, `log_metric`); what's missing is the **self-serve flow** that lets a general user do it without an operator/coach driving every write.

The flow should encode the procedure we ran by hand:
1. **Bind** — upload/paste a doc (or link); attach it as a goal **reference**, auto-generating the `claudeSummary` distillation.
2. **Classify** — detect the doc's kind: *canon/identity* (→ operating rule / filter), *executable plan* (→ targets + scheduled items + metrics), or *reference library* (→ pull-from only, no schedule).
3. **Propose** — surface a translation (proposed targets, scheduled items, operating filter) for the user to approve/edit — never auto-write side-effects without confirmation (propose-before-applying).
4. **Apply** — on approval, write references / scheduled items / targets via the existing tools.
5. **Living docs** — re-uploading an updated doc updates the existing reference (`update_goal_reference`, re-distill summary) instead of creating duplicates.

## Acceptance criteria
- Captured as a backlog item; not designed for v1.
- When designed: A and B share the same in-app "feed this goal" surface; both reuse the existing reference / schedule_item / target write tools rather than new write paths.
- B's apply step is gated by an approval/preview step (no silent side-effects); doc classification (canon vs plan vs library) is surfaced and user-overridable.
- Re-applying an updated doc updates the existing reference, not a duplicate.

## Related
- Manual precedent: rhino.the.grey goal seeded with 3 strategy-doc references + 7 scheduled items by hand (2026-06-28/29).
- Depends on nothing new — it's a UX/flow layer over shipped MCP primitives.

content.body:
## Problem (user-reported)

Goals are silos today: activating the 5k goal deactivates Elbert's plan entirely, and on the 5k's own **race day** the day page shows a generic *Full Body Calisthenics* prescription with no mention of the race. Goaldmine's actual promise is a cumulative tracker + dynamically tailored plan across multiple related-or-unrelated goals — **with realistic expectations**.

## Direction (refined 2026-06-10 interview)

- **App surfaces, coach merges** (standing principle preserved): the app gains multi-goal *awareness* and flags collisions loudly; the claude.ai coach resolves them conversationally via overrides/revisions. No deterministic auto-planning baked into the app.
- **Focus plan + all goals' events**: one goal remains the "focus" driving the daily prescription; every active goal's key dates (race day, summit day, retests, planned hikes, target-date pins) and conflicts render goal-tagged on Today / calendar / day pages.
- **Rarity score (hybrid)**: app computes a deterministic feasibility signal per goal and for the whole stack (readiness trajectory vs days remaining; required vs observed rate of improvement — a 135→315 lb bench in 12 weeks should scream); coach can calibrate the rating with a written rationale via MCP. Displayed as game-standard tiers **Common / Uncommon / Rare / Epic / Legendary** on /character (hero identity) and /goals; Legendary ⇒ explicit "near-impossible in the time set" warning, including at goal creation.
- **Guided intake**: goal creation gets an interview process (coach-led) so expectations are set before a goal enters the stack.

## Phases (each is its own /feature-dev run)

- [ ] #62 — Phase 1: Multi-goal awareness (kills the race-day invisibility bug)
- [ ] #63 — Phase 2: Rarity / feasibility engine + coach calibration
- [ ] #64 — Phase 3: Guided goal intake interview

Build priority relative to other work: after day-page editing (#65) and barcode nutrition (#66).

## Architecture notes for feature-dev

- `Goal.active` is currently a single-focus flag; `setActiveGoal` deactivates all other goals AND their plans (`src/lib/goal-actions.ts:115`). Phase 1 must distinguish *focus* (drives prescription) from *active* (participates in awareness/rarity).
- `ScheduledItem` model (milestone|task|review, goal-linked) already exists — candidate carrier for race/summit events.
- Readiness series (`computeReadinessSeries`) is the trajectory input for rarity.
- The game layer's rule-pack registry is goal-kind-keyed — rarity display belongs beside it on /character.
- Principle on record: "Plan is conversational, not auto-resolved" — do not bake training decisions into deterministic code.

## North-star story (user, 2026-06-10)

> During Full Body Calisthenics I audibled: wall handstand push-ups instead of the prescribed DB shoulder press. I noted that I have an unmeasured goal of one day achieving a handstand — but it remained unrecorded as a goal. The coach said it wasn't far-fetched, wouldn't pull me off the Elbert path with my numbers, and that it could start weaving handstand skill work into my existing workouts. **That kind of integration — programming that knocks out multiple goals at once — is exactly what Goaldmine is for.** Physical or an entirely different category: one daily tailored plan that refines focus and achieves the user's goals with the coach's help.

This story adds three requirements the phases must honor:

1. **Aspirational (unmeasured, undated) goals.** "Achieve a handstand one day" has no targetDate and no metrics yet — the Goal model currently requires a date. Support a `someday`/aspirational goal state: capturable in seconds, visible in the stack, excluded from (or softly weighted in) rarity until it gains a date/targets, and available for the coach to weave into programming. (Lands in Phase 3 scope, with model groundwork allowed in Phase 1.)
2. **One woven plan, not parallel plans.** The end-state is NOT N plans side by side — it's the focus plan absorbing secondary goals' work via coach revisions (skill blocks folded into existing days). The app's job: make every goal visible to the coach (Phase 1 awareness + MCP parity) and attribute woven work to the goals it serves (e.g. handstand-progression sets visibly advance the handstand goal on /goals and in the game layer). Plan-weaving itself stays conversational (apply_plan_revision / overrides) — never deterministic.
3. **Note → goal promotion.** An audible/journal note that contains an aspiration must be promotable into a goal (mirror the existing note→standing_rule promotion pipeline: list_promotable_notes / promote_note). The handstand note should have become a someday-goal in one step.

content.body:
Part of Epic #61 · **Phase 1 of 3** · Priority: build after day-page editing & barcode nutrition.

## Problem

The 5k goal's race day shows a generic *Full Body Calisthenics* prescription with zero mention of the race. Non-focus goals are invisible: their target dates, planned hikes, and retests don't exist on Today, the calendar, or day pages.

## Scope (decided in interview)

**Focus plan + all goals' events.** One goal remains the "focus" driving the daily prescription (current behavior). Every *active* goal additionally contributes goal-tagged awareness:

1. **Decouple focus from active.** `setActiveGoal` currently deactivates all other goals and their plans (`src/lib/goal-actions.ts:115`). Introduce a distinct focus concept; multiple goals can be active (= tracked, visible, counted in rarity later) while one is focused.
2. **Goal events surface everywhere.** Each active goal's key dates — target date ("RACE DAY", "SUMMIT DAY" per goal flavor/legend), scheduled baselines/retests, planned hikes — render on:
   - the calendar (goal-tagged markers; reuse the legend system),
   - Today (an "also today / this week" strip when another goal has an event),
   - day detail pages (e.g. race day shows the race banner above the focus prescription).
3. **Cross-goal conflict flags.** Extend the existing conflict machinery (`weekConflicts`: long-effort, retest-on-hike) to cross-goal collisions: another goal's event lands on a hard training day, two goals' key events same week, etc. Surface loudly; never auto-resolve (coach resolves via overrides — standing principle).
4. **MCP parity.** `get_today_plan` / `get_day` / `get_week` / `get_session_brief` include other active goals' events + conflicts so the coach sees exactly what the app shows.

## Out of scope

Merged daily prescriptions (rejected), rarity (Phase 2, #61), guided intake (Phase 3, #61), any deterministic plan merging.

## Acceptance sketch

- With 5k active (focus = Elbert): 5k race day on /calendar shows a goal-tagged race marker; /days/<race-date> shows a race banner; Today (race week) surfaces it; get_day(race-date) includes it.
- setFocus(goalB) does not deactivate goalA or its plan.
- Conflict appears when a race lands within N days of a long-effort/hike day.

content.body:
Part of Epic #61 · **Phase 2 of 3** · Depends on Phase 1 (multiple active goals).

## Concept

A "rarity" score answering: *how hard is it to reach ALL current goals within their timelines?* Per-goal feasibility + a stack-level rating, in game-standard tiers: **Common / Uncommon / Rare / Epic / Legendary**. Legendary = "near-impossible in the time set" with an explicit warning. Example from the interview: training for Elbert + a 5k, then adding "bench 315 lb" with a recorded 135 lb PR ⇒ Legendary, warn the user.

## Engine (hybrid — decided in interview)

1. **Deterministic feasibility signal (app)** per active goal: required rate of improvement vs observed rate — readiness trajectory (`computeReadinessSeries`) and per-metric trends (records/baselines) projected against days-to-target. Stack score combines per-goal feasibility + concurrent-load factor (training time collisions from Phase 1 conflict data). All thresholds in one tunable rules module (mirror `src/lib/game/rules.ts` pattern; goal-kind-aware via the rule-pack registry).
2. **Coach calibration (MCP)**: `set_goal_feasibility(goalId, tier, rationale)` write tool — coach can override/calibrate the computed tier with a written rationale (shown in UI: "Coach assessed: Epic — recovering from injury"). Computed value remains visible alongside. Read surface: include rarity in `get_goal` / `list_goals` / `get_game_state`.

## Display

- **/character**: rarity chip in the hero identity area (bullseye-brand styling, no cartoon), per the game layer's design language; both themes; tokens only.
- **/goals**: per-goal tier + the stack rating; **Legendary banner** ("This stack is near-impossible in the time set — talk to your coach or adjust timelines") on /goals and at goal creation.
- Warning fires at creation time when adding the goal would push the stack to Epic/Legendary.

## Out of scope

Auto-adjusting plans or timelines (coach's job); rarity for non-fitness goal kinds beyond the registry fallback (note the seam, don't build packs).

## Acceptance sketch

- Deterministic: same data ⇒ same tiers; thresholds in one module; unit-sane on the 135→315 bench example (Legendary).
- set_goal_feasibility validates tier, stores rationale, surfaces in app + read tools.
- Legendary stack shows warnings on /goals and at creation. MCP connector reload reminder in ship report (tool surface changes).

content.body:
Part of Epic #61 · **Phase 3 of 3** · Depends on Phase 2 (rarity at intake).

## Concept

"Wondering if there should be an interview process" — yes: goal creation becomes a guided intake so a goal enters the stack with realistic expectations, benchmarks, and a coach-designed plan, instead of a bare objective + date.

## Scope (coach-led, app-assisted — consistent with no-LLM-in-app)

1. **Coach interview flow**: a structured goal-intake prompt card on /coach ("Start a new goal interview") that walks the user through: objective, hard date vs flexible, current benchmarks relevant to the goal (logged as baselines during the interview), constraints (equipment, schedule, concurrent goals), and produces: targets + weights, a plan proposal, and a Phase-2 rarity preview *before* the goal is committed.
2. **MCP support**: whatever the interview needs that's missing — e.g. a `preview_goal_feasibility(targets, targetDate)` read tool that computes the would-be rarity of the stack WITH the candidate goal, without creating it. Audit create_goal inputs for anything the interview produces that can't be stored.
3. **App-side intake entry**: the /goals create form gains a prominent "Interview with your coach first (recommended)" path (deep link / copy-prompt), while keeping direct creation for quick goals. If direct creation would produce an Epic/Legendary stack, show the Phase-2 warning with a "consider the interview" nudge.

## Out of scope

In-app chat or LLM calls (reasoning stays in claude.ai); auto-generated plans without coach involvement.

## Acceptance sketch

- Prompt card produces a complete interview when pasted in claude.ai (tested against the live coach with the MCP connector).
- preview_goal_feasibility returns a tier without persisting anything.
- /goals create form shows the interview path + stack warning integration.


## Scope addition (north-star story, see Epic #61)

4. **Aspirational goal capture + note→goal promotion.** Not every goal arrives through a full interview. Two lightweight entry paths:
   - **Someday goals**: create a goal with no targetDate and no targets (objective only, e.g. "Achieve a freestanding handstand"). Schema: targetDate becomes optional (or a `someday` status), excluded from / softly weighted in rarity until dated, listed distinctly on /goals ("Someday" section). The interview is the upgrade path: when ready, the coach interviews to add date + targets and the goal joins the measured stack.
   - **Promote a note to a goal**: an audible/journal note containing an aspiration ("unmeasured goal: handstand one day") becomes a someday-goal in one step — mirror the existing note→standing_rule pipeline (`list_promotable_notes` / `promote_note`) with a `promote_note_to_goal` MCP tool + an app-side affordance on /journal pending notes.
   - **Woven-work attribution** (coordinate with Phase 1): when the coach weaves skill work for a someday-goal into the focus plan (e.g. handstand-progression sets), that work should be attributable to the goal — at minimum via exercise→goal hints in goal legend/targets — so /goals and the game layer show the aspiration is actually being trained.

content.body:
Priority 1 of the 2026-06-10 improvements batch (build first).

## Problem

A past day with no logged workout is a dead end: the day page says "No workout logged — Import one or log via Claude" and offers nothing else. The app can't log a workout, mark a day as skipped/rest, log a hike, or edit an already-logged workout — all of that currently requires Strong import or the coach.

## Scope (all four selected in interview)

1. **Manual workout logger** on /days/[dateKey] (any date, past or today): exercises + sets (reps / weight / duration), **prefilled from that day's resolved prescription** (override-aware via resolveDay — logging should mostly be confirming what was prescribed, with edits). Saving creates a normal Workout (status completed, source "manual") → feeds PRs, records, XP exactly like imported workouts (recordsSet detection + canonicalExerciseName).
2. **Mark day skipped / rest**: one-tap status for past gaps so the adherence record (and the game layer's streak/ledger) is honest. Decide representation with care: a status="skipped" Workout row vs a day-level marker — must play correctly with the game engine's day ledger (skipped planned workout breaks streak by design; an acknowledged rest/sick day is information, not automatically forgiveness — surface the distinction, let the coach rule on exceptions per the conversational-plan principle).
3. **Inline hike logging** on the day page: route, distance, elevation, pack weight, duration, RPE → Hike row (same shape as log_hike MCP).
4. **Edit a logged workout** in-app: from /workouts/[id], edit sets/reps/weights/duration (parity with update_workout_set / workout_ops MCP capabilities). Server actions with revalidatePath on /, /history, /days/[dateKey], /workouts/[id].

## Constraints

- Mobile-first 390px; forms thumb-reachable; reuse Card/form patterns and the new ConfirmButton for destructive actions (no window.confirm — iOS PWA).
- All dates via @/lib/calendar; workouts compared by startedAt (preserve time-of-day semantics; manual logs default to a sensible time).
- Server components by default; client islands only for the form interactivity.
- XP/records side-effects must match MCP log_workout behavior (single code path preferred — reuse the same lib functions, don't fork logic).

## Acceptance sketch

- Past empty day → log a prefilled workout in ≤4 taps for an as-prescribed session; PRs + XP fire; day page and Today reflect immediately.
- Skipped/rest marking visible on calendar + day page and honored sensibly by the streak ledger.
- Hike logged in-app appears identically to a coach-logged hike.
- Editing a set in-app updates records/XP consistently (derived engine recomputes automatically).

content.body:
Priority 2 of the 2026-06-10 improvements batch (after day-page editing, before multi-goal).

## Problem

Logging a meal with macros means typing every number. Original ask: scan a nutrition label and have macros calculated, keeping macros optional when nothing is scanned.

## Approach (decided in interview): barcode + food database + personal food library

No LLM calls in the app ($0 constraint), so instead of OCR: scan the **barcode** with the phone camera, look up the product in **OpenFoodFacts** (free API), auto-fill per-serving macros (calories, protein, carbs, fat, fiber, sodium — the existing MACRO_KEYS), user sets quantity/servings, macros scale. Everything stays editable and optional, exactly as today.

## Scope

1. **Scanner**: in the Log sheet's meal form, a "Scan barcode" affordance opening a camera view. Use the native `BarcodeDetector` API where available with a wasm fallback (e.g. zxing-wasm) — **iOS Safari/PWA is the primary platform and lacks BarcodeDetector; the fallback is the main path, so weigh its bundle size and load it lazily.** Camera permission flow + manual barcode-number entry fallback.
2. **Lookup**: OpenFoodFacts product endpoint → name, brand, serving size, per-100g + per-serving macros. Handle not-found gracefully (keep manual entry). Decide proxying: client-direct vs a thin /api route (CORS, rate limits — OpenFoodFacts allows client calls; verify).
3. **Personal food library** (new Prisma model): every successful lookup saves {barcode, name, brand, servingSize, macros}; meal form gets a quick-pick of recent/frequent library foods so daily staples are one tap, no re-scan. Library hits skip the network.
4. **Macro math**: item quantity × per-serving macros aggregates into the NutritionLog macro fields (same shape the coach writes via log_nutrition). Mixed meals (scanned + manual items) sum what's known; missing macros stay null, never fabricated.
5. **MCP parity (light)**: coach read access to the food library if cheap (e.g. include frequent foods in get_nutrition_history context) — optional, don't force it.

## Constraints

- Camera/scan UI is a client island; the rest stays server-rendered. Tokens-only styling; BottomSheet patterns for the scan view.
- Migration is additive (Neon shared with prod).
- Scan failure / no camera / denied permission must degrade to today's manual flow with zero friction.

## Acceptance sketch

- Scan a real packaged food on iPhone PWA → macros auto-filled, quantity adjustable, logged meal totals correct.
- Same product next time appears in quick-pick without scanning.
- No-match barcode → graceful manual path. Lighthouse/bundle check: scanner code lazy-loaded, not in the base bundle.

**Epic:** Sprint 8 — Feasibility surface
**Value:** so that any goal's feasibility can be rendered honestly in the dashboard from a single GoalFeasibility, without an MCP round-trip and without re-hardcoding a vertical

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New file src/components/FeasibilityReadout.tsx is a server component (no "use client" directive, no React hooks/state).
- [ ] Component accepts an already-serialized GoalFeasibility prop; it never accepts or constructs a Date instance (GoalFeasibility.computedAt is already a string, weeksRemaining a number) — no Date crosses the server/client boundary.
- [ ] Renders exactly four honest states selected purely from the GoalFeasibility value: (1) unratedReason==="someday" -> "No deadline set — feasibility unrated."; (2) unratedReason==="no-targets" -> "Add targets to rate feasibility."; (3) unratedReason==="no-data" (see two sub-states below); (4) tier set -> full readout with per-target requiredRate vs observedRate, weeksRemaining, verdict tier, and rateBasis.
- [ ] The no-data state renders TWO distinct sub-states keyed on requiredRate===null: 0-log sub-state (requiredRate===null AND observedRate===null) -> copy can only say e.g. "Not enough logged data to rate yet — log the metric a few times" / "Add at least one entry to compute the pace you need" (it must NOT promise a per-week target, because requiredRate is null at zero logs per plan-critique C-1); 1–2-log sub-state (requiredRate populated, observedRate null) -> shows requiredRate (e.g. "needs ~$66/wk to hit $1,000 by 9/30") with observedRate rendered as "— not yet estimable".
- [ ] Goal-generic: zero hardcoded references to fitness, Elbert, Chewgether, MRR, or any vertical-specific label — all labels derive from the GoalFeasibility perTarget entries.
- [ ] Any date label (e.g. target date) is formatted via @/lib/calendar USER_TZ helpers, never a raw new Date()/toLocaleString without timeZone.
- [ ] NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts: git diff --stat shows src/lib/rarity-core.ts is untouched by this story.
- [ ] Chewgether criterion: rendered against the live Chewgether GoalFeasibility (0 logs) the component shows 'not enough logged data to rate' honestly today; rendered against a >=3-MRR-log fixture GoalFeasibility it shows a real verdict (tier + rateBasis "observed").
- [ ] npx tsc --noEmit and npm run lint pass.

content.body:
**Epic:** Sprint 9 — Honesty-math tests
**Value:** so that the readiness score is provably honest — a partially-logged or untested goal can never display a false 100%, gating targets verifiably cap the headline at 80, and every metric direction is scored correctly.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New file src/lib/readiness.test.ts; `npx vitest run` is green
- [ ] progressFor: build-from-zero increase metrics (log:*, hike:*, workout:count) return current/target (covers the no-start accumulation path); target===0 returns null
- [ ] progressFor: already-met case — increase current>=target returns 1, decrease current<=target returns 1 (even when start is below target)
- [ ] progressFor: decrease comparative metric returns clamped (start-current)/(start-target); start===target returns 0; current===null returns null (untested)
- [ ] computeReadiness: an open gating target forces ceiling=80 and score=Math.min(rawScore,80) — covers the gating-cap → ceiling 80 case; all gates cleared yields ceiling=100
- [ ] computeReadiness: an untested target counts as 0 progress at full weight in the denominator (covers untested=0 — asserts NO false-100% when one of two equally-weighted targets is unlogged)
- [ ] computeReadiness: coverage returns {tested,total} where tested counts only breakdown rows with progress!==null; missing[] and openGateCount are correct
- [ ] computeReadiness: totalWeight===0 returns score 0 / rawScore 0 (no NaN)
- [ ] resolveMetricValue/resolveMetricStart from @/lib/goal-targets are mocked (vi.mock) so cases are deterministic without a live DB; goalId/asOf are passed but never hit Prisma

- `src/lib/readiness.test.ts`

content.body:
**Epic:** Sprint 9 — Honesty-math tests
**Value:** so that the feasibility verdict is provably honest for any numeric goal — a thin data set reads 'unknown / does not count' instead of a confident tier, a stalled metric reads as a fantasy rather than easy, and decrease metrics are not silently inverted.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New file src/lib/rarity-core.test.ts; `npx vitest run` is green
- [ ] Tests the EXISTING engine (computeTargetFeasibility in rarity-core.ts) — pure function, no source change and no mocking required
- [ ] log: observed path: observedPoints>=3 with positive observedWeeklyRate (null norm family) yields rateBasis 'observed', plausibleRate===observedWeeklyRate, a real numeric ratio and a non-unknown verdict, countsTowardTier:true
- [ ] Honest <3-points path: observedPoints of 0/1/2 with a null-norm family returns verdict 'unknown', countsTowardTier:false, ratio null, rateBasis 'none' (asserts minObservedPoints stays 3 — no false confidence from a 2-point slope)
- [ ] Met-check: gap<=0 (current already at/past target, both directions) returns verdict 'met', ratio 0, requiredRate 0, countsTowardTier:true
- [ ] ratioCap stall path: observedPoints>=3 with observedWeeklyRate<=0 and null norm returns ratio===ratioCap (99), verdict 'legendary', countsTowardTier:true (stalled = effectively impossible)
- [ ] Decrease-sign normalization: a decrease metric passed a pre-normalized positive observedWeeklyRate is treated as improving (real ratio, not regression) — documents the caller's negate convention
- [ ] Never-measured guard: current===null with no explicit target.start returns verdict 'unknown', countsTowardTier:false, requiredRate null

content.body:
**[A2 · Epic A · Sprint 1]**

**Value:** so the GPU-box Claude worker can queue/list/claim/draft/render/complete render jobs over MCP

**Acceptance criteria:**
- New registerRenderTools(server) with SIX tools: queue_render_job(date, clipforgeProjectId [required in zod], goalId? -> defaults to current FOCUS goal, error if none) UPSERT (re-queue terminal job -> pending); list_render_jobs(status?, from?, to?); claim_render_job(id) ATOMIC updateMany({where:{id,status:'pending'},data:{status:'claimed',claimedAt}}) returns claimed:boolean from count; start_render_job(id) ATOMIC updateMany({where:{id,status:'approved'},data:{status:'rendering',claimedAt}}) returns started:boolean (the render-run claim, M1); submit_render_draft(id,{draftRef,notes}) -> 'drafted'; complete_render_job(id,{outputRef,status:'rendered'|'failed', errorMessage?})
- EXPLICIT AC: registerRenderTools(server) wired into registerAll() (else tools silently absent)
- EXPLICIT AC: confirm the new tools appear in tools/list against the live deploy (MCP_SERVER_VERSION auto-bumps via commit SHA — verify connector discovers them)
- All safe()-wrapped; dates via parseDateInput (USER_TZ); discoverable descriptions steering the worker
- MCP curl: queue -> list -> claim (2nd claim returns claimed:false) -> submit_render_draft -> (approve via UI) -> start_render_job (2nd returns started:false) -> complete_render_job round-trips

**Touches:** src/lib/mcp/tools/render-tools.ts (new), src/lib/mcp/tools.ts (registerAll wiring)

**Epic:** Generic metric semantics · **Finding #1** · **Story A1**

**Value:** so a cumulative goal (practice hours, books read, miles) accumulates correctly instead of reading only the latest entry — which today both understates progress AND mistiers easy goals as Epic (two independent bugs: wrong current value + zero observed rate).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] GoalTarget gains cumulative?:boolean AND it is added to GoalTargetSchema (zod) so write tools accept it; default false → MRR/followers/weight unchanged
- [ ] resolveMetricValue SUMs LogEntry.value over the range when cumulative; resolveMetricStart returns 0
- [ ] Cumulative log: metrics use the weekly-cumulative-snapshot rate branch (reuse hike:/workout:count path) so observed rate = weekly accumulation, not a ~0 per-session slope
- [ ] DATA BACKFILL: set cumulative:true on the existing guitar target via update_goal_targets (the existing targets JSON row is unchanged by a type edit alone — planJson-snapshot rule)
- [ ] Guitar 100-hr goal: current=sum of sessions AND Reach tier is no longer Epic
- [ ] typecheck + build green; existing snapshot-metric tests unchanged; new test for the cumulative path

**Touches:** GoalTarget type + GoalTargetSchema (zod — gates create_goal, promote_note_to_goal, update_goal_targets, preview; without it the coach cannot set the flag), src/lib/goal-targets.ts (resolveMetricValue ~105-117 SUM path, resolveMetricStart 0-path), src/lib/rarity.ts (route cumulative log: into the weekly-cumulative-snapshot branch ~103-123 that hike:/workout:count use, not the per-entry slope branch ~125-137), computeGoalFeasibility current fallback. NOTE: targets is a JSON column — no Prisma migration.

**Depends on:** none
**Priority:** P1 - High · **Effort:** Large · **Sprint:** Sprint 9 - Honesty-math tests

**Epic:** Trends data spine · **Initiative phase:** Sprint 1 · **Story A1**

**Value:** so any log: metric can be charted over time — the reusable data spine the whole trends UI sits on.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] getLogMetricSeries(target: GoalTarget, goalId) returns { points: {date, value, label}[], label, units, domain: [number,number] }
- [ ] SNAPSHOT path (target.cumulative falsy): flat-map LogEntry rows (metric = target.metric minus 'log:'), date asc; latest-wins for same-day duplicates
- [ ] CUMULATIVE path (target.cumulative true, per A1/#132): group rows by USER_TZ day -> sum within day -> prefix-sum across days = accumulation curve; same-day collapse is required
- [ ] domain rule: cumulative -> [0, max*1.1]; units '%' -> [0,100]; snapshot -> padded dataMin/dataMax (BodyMetricsSection pattern)
- [ ] label/units from the metrics registry, falling back to target.label / target.units (practice_hours/followers are NOT in the registry)
- [ ] each point carries a pre-formatted USER_TZ date label (chart must NOT re-format the ISO instant client-side)
- [ ] unit test for both paths incl. multi-same-day-rows; tsc + lint + vitest green

**Value:** so that a user comparing dates sees the exact same readiness number for 'today' that /progress shows, instead of a stale end-of-day snapshot that silently diverges from live data (CLAUDE.md invariant: compare and /progress must share the same computeReadiness path)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] src/lib/compare.ts computes a single wall-clock read (const now = new Date(); const todayKey = toDateKey(now)) and derives asOfA/asOfB = date===todayKey ? now : endOfDay(parseDateKey(date)), used ONLY at the two computeReadiness call sites inside buildGoalSections (function takes 4 date args: cutA, cutB, asOfA, asOfB); every other section (strength, baselines, body, counters) keeps existing endOfDay(cutA)/endOfDay(cutB) snapshot semantics unchanged
- [ ] new regression test in src/lib/compare.test.ts asserts that when dateB is today, computeComparison's per-goal readiness for that goal equals the same value src/app/progress/page.tsx:41 (computeReadiness(targets, new Date(), g.id)) would compute — proving byte-identical parity; test must fail against the pre-fix code path and pass after
- [ ] MCP curl smoke of compare_dates (POST /api/mcp per README 'MCP server' smoke recipe, body {a: <30-days-ago>, b: <today>}) captured before and after the change, showing today's per-goal readiness score matches the equivalent get_today_plan/compute_readiness value for the same goal
- [ ] src/lib/mcp/tools.ts compare_dates tool description (~line 1230-1235) updated to document the today carve-out — it currently states pure 'latest-known value ≤ end of each day' semantics, which is no longer true for a date that equals today and must say so explicitly
- [ ] as-of microcopy line added near the /compare hero (in src/app/compare/page.tsx or src/components/compare/HeroSpan.tsx) communicating the snapshot semantic ('as of end of day, except today which is live') — verified via 390px render
- [ ] npm run test and npx tsc --noEmit both clean

**Touches:** `src/lib/compare.ts`, `src/lib/compare.test.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tools.ts`, `src/app/compare/page.tsx`, `src/components/compare/HeroSpan.tsx`

content.body:
**Value:** so that project-kind goals (e.g. a business launch) stop showing fitness-framed readiness cards, workout/hike counters, and a raw 'project' class label — all copy and computation should be goal-kind-aware for multi-domain users

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] src/app/goals/[id]/page.tsx:114's readiness computation is gated to goal.kind === "fitness" && targets.length > 0 (was targets.length > 0 alone); the existing JSX guard at line 237 ({readiness && ...}) then hides the fitness readiness card entirely for project goals with no further change needed there
- [ ] 'The work between' Card in src/app/compare/page.tsx is rendered only when result.goals.some(g => g.kind === "fitness") is true (not gameState.goalKind, which breaks multi-domain users); a compare run with only project-kind goals shows no fitness workout/hike/ft counters
- [ ] GoalPresentation type in src/lib/goal-presentation.ts gains a new classLabel: string field; FITNESS_PRESENTATION.classLabel = "Adventurer", PROJECT_PRESENTATION.classLabel = "Builder", DEFAULT_PRESENTATION inherits FITNESS_PRESENTATION's classLabel via its existing spread
- [ ] src/app/character/page.tsx:76 replaces the inline ternary (state.goalKind === "fitness" ? "Adventurer" : state.goalKind) with the resolved classLabel from presentationForGoal({ kind: state.goalKind }); a project-focus character page shows 'Builder' instead of the raw string 'project'
